pairs.subject: Mandatory Training Session
pairs.desc:
Dear Employee,

We are implementing a new training program that is mandatory for all staff. This training will cover important updates regarding our company policies and procedures. 

Please find the attached document that outlines the training schedule and topics. Make sure to review it carefully and mark your calendars.

If you have any questions, feel free to reach out.

Best regards,
HR Team
pairs.explain: The email is from the HR department, includes a legitimate attachment, and is informative about mandatory training.

pairs.subject: Urgent: Training Document Update
pairs.desc:
Dear Employee,

We have updated the training document that you need to review immediately. It is crucial for compliance with new regulations. 

Please download the attached file and complete the review by the end of the day.

Thank you,
HR Team
pairs.explain: The email contains an executable attachment, which is a common tactic in phishing attempts.

pairs.subject: Quarterly Financial Report
pairs.desc:
Hello Team,

Attached is the quarterly financial report for your review. Please ensure to go through it before our next meeting. If you have any questions or need further clarification, do not hesitate to ask.

Best,
Finance Department
pairs.explain: The email is from the finance department with a legitimate PDF attachment containing a financial report.

pairs.subject: Important: Financial Document
pairs.desc:
Dear Employee,

Please find the attached financial document that requires your immediate attention. It is important to process this as soon as possible.

Regards,
Finance Team
pairs.explain: The email contains an executable attachment, which is suspicious and indicative of a phishing attempt.

pairs.subject: IT System Maintenance Notice
pairs.desc:
Dear Team,

We would like to inform you that there will be scheduled maintenance on our IT systems this weekend. Please save your work and log off by 6 PM on Friday.

Thank you for your cooperation.

Best,
IT Department
pairs.explain: The email is a standard notification from the IT department with no attachments, providing clear information.

pairs.subject: Urgent: IT System Update Required
pairs.desc:
Dear Employee,

We need you to update your system immediately. Please download the attached file and run it to ensure your system is secure.

Thank you,
IT Team
pairs.explain: The email requests the download of a batch file, which is a common phishing tactic to compromise systems.

pairs.subject: Travel Expense Reimbursement
pairs.desc:
Hi,

Attached you will find the reimbursement form for your recent travel expenses. Please fill it out and submit it by the end of the week to ensure timely processing.

Best,
Travel Department
pairs.explain: The email is from the travel department with a legitimate PDF attachment for reimbursement.

pairs.subject: Action Required: Travel Expense Document
pairs.desc:
Dear Employee,

We have an important document regarding your travel expenses that needs your immediate action. Please download and execute the attached file to proceed.

Regards,
Travel Team
pairs.explain: The email contains an executable attachment, which is a red flag for phishing attempts.

pairs.subject: Security Policy Update
pairs.desc:
Hello Team,

We have updated our security policies to enhance data protection. Please review the updated policies on our internal portal and ensure compliance.

Thank you,
Security Team
pairs.explain: The email is a standard communication about policy updates from the security department with no attachments.

pairs.subject: Urgent: Security Policy Document
pairs.desc:
Dear Employee,

Please find the attached document that requires your urgent attention regarding our security policy. Download and execute the file to ensure compliance.

Best,
Security Team
pairs.explain: The email contains an executable attachment, which is a common phishing tactic.
